comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout. AgentWorker constructs WorkerSessionController with spawnBackground: (effect) => Effect.runFork(effect) at extensions/tau/src/agent/worker.ts:72-83, so worker background tasks are launched on the default runtime rather than a worker/extension-owned scope. WorkerSessionController.attach() triggers reprompts via this.options.spawnBackground(this.replaceBackground(this.options.repromptForSubmitResult(retry))) at extensions/tau/src/agent/worker/session-controller.ts:40-43. replaceBackground(...) only stores the inner fiber created inside its effect (line 57). The outer detached fiber that runs replaceBackground(...) is never tracked. shutdown() only interrupts activeFiber (lines 63-77), so shutdown can miss that outer fiber if it has not yet installed the inner active fiber. That creates a real lifecycle race: a late outer fiber can still spawn a reprompt after shutdown/release. The broader structured-concurrency concern is also valid because runFork is detached from the worker lifecycle.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Related architectural verification: the worker split is helpful but incomplete. Lifecycle ownership still leaks through AgentWorker via spawnBackground: (effect) => Effect.runFork(effect) in extensions/tau/src/agent/worker.ts:72-83, so WorkerSessionController is not the sole owner of worker background resources. Session/event state also still mutates a shared mutable WorkerTrackingState object directly from subscription callbacks in extensions/tau/src/agent/worker/session-subscription.ts:71-148, rather than being encapsulated behind a scoped service/resource boundary. This is not a separate compile break, but it supports the broader point that file decomposition did not fully become ownership decomposition.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Implemented fix in worker runtime/session control: AgentWorker now receives runtime-backed runFork from AgentRuntimeBridge/AgentManager instead of calling Effect.runFork directly, so background worker fibers are launched through the tau runtime bridge. WorkerSessionController reprompt path now uses a synchronous background replacement path (replaceBackgroundSync) instead of spawning an outer detached fiber around replaceBackground(...), removing the shutdown race where a late outer fiber could launch a post-shutdown reprompt. Added regression coverage in test/agent-worker-session-controller.test.ts for delayed-spawn shutdown behavior, and updated affected worker wiring/constructor tests to the new runFork parameter shape.
